observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the CENRO in furnishing the Audit Team with the notarized copy of the MOA and submit documents and justifications for the deviation from the MOA for further evaluation and explain why the disbursement of P294,500.00 should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The CENRO furnished the notarized MOA to the Audit Team and submitted justification explaining the reasons for deviation from the MOA. Documents were provided for review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

a. reconstitute Inventory Committee due to the resignation from office of one of
its members and instruct the new members to fully participate in the conduct
of the physical count of all property of the City within the time table to
accomplish the inventory taking and report preparation;

b. City Accountant and the City GSO to reconcile their records and thoroughly
evaluate the causes of discrepancies and complete the preparation of
inventory records for all PPE accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE
balances inthefinancial statements; and

c. instruct the City GSO, in coordination with the Inventory Committee and
Local School Board, to prepare and submit a duly accomplished RPCPPE for
allPPE items purchased by theCityunder all funds.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant draw JEV
debiting Accounts Payable and crediting Construction in Progress both by
P282,695,946.92 tocorrect the balance of the accounts.

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
recognize liabilities only when the goods and services are accepted or rendered
and supplier/creditor bills are received in compliance with Section 4(s) of the
MNGAS forLGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to fast track
the review of the reported amounts and projects in the Registry of Public
Infrastructure and once completed, record back to the books the Infrastructure
Assets that were previously transferred to the RPI with their corresponding
accumulated depreciation by using the appropriate accounts prescribed in the
Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs. Henceforth, recognize in the books of
accounts the Infrastructure Assets andprovide depreciation forthesame.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Engineer to provide the
City Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects to facilitate the proper disclosure of the road
network system inthe Notes toFinancial Statements by theCity Accountant.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to review all the
accounting entries that affect both accounts, determine the cause/s of the
discrepancy between their balances, after which make the necessary adjustments
to bring into agreement the balances of the reciprocal accounts to ensure fair
presentation of theaccounts inthefinancial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to furnish the
City Accountant at the beginning of the year of a duly certified list showing the
name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will
serve as basis to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in recording the debit to
Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and credit to
DeferredReal Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to review the
transactions under RPT/SET Receivables account and make necessary
adjustments, ifwarranted.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to reconcile the
RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the City Treasurer’s list of RPT/SET
delinquencies and make necessary adjustments, if any, to present fairly the
financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor City Accountant to draw a Journal Entry
Voucher to adjust the account in the Trust Fund books by debiting Trust
Liability – DRRM account and crediting Due to Other Funds totaling P2,582,020.45
and to adjust the General Fund books by debiting Due from Other Funds and
crediting PriorPeriod Adjustmenttotaling P2,582,020.45.

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and the
City Treasurer to transfer from the Trust Fund to General Fund books the over-
transfer of theDRRM funds totaling P2,733,235.79.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
  City Accountant to:
a. maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards for each type of supplies and materials;
and
b. record the issuances based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) instead of theRIS; and
  CityGeneral Services Officer(CGSO) andother concerned personnel to:
a. maintain Stock Cards foreach type ofsupplies;
b. prepare and submit monthly the SSMI to the City Accountant for the
recording of issuances; and
c. prepare and submit the RPCI to report the physical count of supplies by
type of inventory as of a given date.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct theCity Accountant to:

a. review MOA/Contracts/Certificates or similar documents on the guaranty
deposits withvarious utility companies;
b. exert extra efforts to expedite the retrieval of old records to establish the
details of the reported OtherReceivables andDue toNGAs account;
c. request for write-off of long overdue receivables to determine the present
value andimpairment loss; and
d. liquidate the unsupported balances of Due to NGAs to the Bureau of Treasury,
incase thedetails of the account cannot bedetermined.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a. City Accountant to ensure that all collections in the custody of the Treasury
at year-end be accounted as Cash - Local Treasury and only record in Cash in
Bank - Local Currency, Checking Account (LCCA) upon actual deposit of the
collections;
b. City Accountant to prepare accounting entries to take up the cancellation of
stale check in the books of accounts of the City by debiting Cash-in-Bank,
Local Currency, Current Account and crediting Accounts Payable in the
amount of P13,966.85 and observe strict compliance to Section 13 of the
MNGAS forLGUS, VolumeI; and
c. City Treasurer to adjust the cashbook by adding the reversal of the
outstanding check totaling P480.00. In addition, prepare and send written
notice to the payees at least one month before a check becomes stale as
required under Section 59 ofthe MNGAS forLGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to reclassify
the accounts by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Biological Assets
in the amount of P574,750.00 to present the correct balance of the account in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to coordinate
with the Provincial Accountant of Laguna to address the discrepancies in the
reciprocal accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs and make the necessary
adjustments.

We recommended thatthe City Mayor:

a. require the Local Finance Committee to study thoroughly the reasonableness
of the projected income toarrive at amore realistic budget; and
b. require the City Treasurer, Head - Assessors Office, Head - BPLO and other
concerned officials to conduct a periodic review of its collection efficiency to
keep track of the revenue accounts that do not meet the revenue targets for
appropriate remedial measures.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to facilitate the immediate submission of the 271 DVs involving a total
amount of P15,985,297.10 as well as the 18 DVs covering CYs 2015 to 2017
transactions amounting to P135,161.10 together with the supporting documents,
otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit. Henceforth,
require submission of accounts within the first 10 days of the ensuing month as
required by COACircular Nos. 2009-006.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the City Mayor direct the City
Accountant andCity Treasurer to:

a. stop granting cash advances to officials, employees or accountable officers
with outstanding cash advances, and to strictly monitor the granting and
utilization of cashadvances;
b. prepare follow-up demand letters to all Accountable Officers (AOs) with
unliquidated cash advances including those AOs who have retired or already
outof service;
c. initiate the request for write-off of accountability for deceased AO in
accordance withthe provisions under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19,2016;
d. request for AO’s authorization (in affidavit form) to deduct all financial
obligations from Terminal Benefit as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001
for AOswhoare no longerintheservice; and
e. strictly enforce the withholding of salaries of AOs for failure to
liquidate/settle their cash advances within the prescribed period in
accordance withSection 100 of PD No. 1445.

Likewise, enforce administrative and criminal sanctions to all AOs who failed to
settle their long overdue cash advances, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002
datedFebruary 10, 1997.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare and
maintain complete subsidiary records of each special account. Accordingly, at the
end of each year, prepare trial balance and financial statements of each special
account incompliancewithSection 110 of the MNGAS forLGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the SPTI Administrator and BUB
Coordinator to coordinate with TESDA Region IV-A and DOLE Region IV-A,
respectively, to put into writing any amendment and modifications from original
MOAand submit the sametothe Audit Team for further verification.

We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned official to explain
or justify why the MOA with TESDA was signed earlier than the approval date of
the authority tosign theMOA.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Human Resource Officer to
adopt the ATM payroll system to safeguard government funds from risk of loss or
misapplication associated withthemanualpayroll system.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to transfer the
payroll preparation and computation, including the updating of remittance records,
to the City Human Resource Management Office for proper internal control
procedure.

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and City
Human Resource Management Office to have close coordination for the
improvement of thepayroll operations oftheCity Government.

We recommended that the Local School Board to reprogram the P20,000,000.00
budget to programs, projects and activities that are in compliant with the legal
and regulatory requirements of the Special Education Fund as stated in Section 4
of DepEd-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2017.

We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City GSO, with the assistance of the Inventory Committee, to continue the conduct of the physical count of all property, exert all efforts to complete the inventory list of all existing PPEs and completely fill out RPCPPE;
b. City GSO to coordinate with all departments/offices of the City to expedite completion of the physical count of PPE under their own custody; and
c. City Accountant to verify, evaluate and address the causes of discrepancies between the Accounting and Property records and complete the preparation of Property Ledger Cards for all PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Conducted physical count of various property of the City.
observations.reason: The physical count of all properties were not completed due to lack of time and lack of personnel to conduct the physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City personnel in-charge to facilitate the transfer of titles of the acquired lands in the name of the City Government of San Pedro to protect the City.
observations.managementAction: A team was created to facilitate the processing of the transfer of titles. Management started collating the documents needed for the transfer of registration.
observations.reason: Parcel of lands were not transferred in the name of the City due to incomplete documentary requirements of the Registry of Deeds, contrary to Section 47(c) of the IRR of RA No. 7160, thus the risk of third party claims and non-establishment of the City’s ownership over the parcel of lands.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake an appraisal of these unserviceable property and prepare a corresponding report thereon; and
b. once the appraisal has been conducted and the documents required are complete, direct the City General Services Officer to submit to the Office of the Auditor a request for disposal of all unserviceable motor vehicles together with IIRUP, Appraisal Report and other required documents for appropriate action.
observations.managementAction: Appraisal of the unserviceable properties were made for 12 motor vehicles and was submitted to COA Office on April 25, 2019.
observations.reason: Remaining 22 motor vehicles were not appraised due to incomplete documentations.

observations.managementAction: The City Accountant was able to submit partially the Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents for the total of P6,003,100.40. The City Accountant minimized the practice of granting cash advances to employees who are not properly bonded. Issuance of demand letters will be made together with enforced liquidation of cash advances by the AOs. Nevertheless, the Management committed to implement strict controls and adherence on the guidelines in granting and liquidation of cash advances such as strictly enforcing the withholding of salaries of all AOs who will fail to liquidate/settle their cash advances.
observations.reason: The physical granting of cash advances continued and the City was not able to strictly observe the submission on time of the liquidation reports for many cash advances.

observations.managementAction: The City Accountant used the Perpetual Inventory Method in recording the purchases of inventory. The City Accountant prepared Subsidiary Ledger Cards and schedules for Supplies and Materials accounts and there is ongoing preparation of the SLC. Maintenance of the records is to be implemented using the SSMI to be submitted by the GSO. Coordination among offices will be in place to ensure that inventory balances presented are reliable.
observations.reason: Verification of records disclosed that the Subsidiary Ledger Cards were not updated as of year-end; SSMI for various inventory accounts were not prepared; some inventory accounts were not covered with Stock Cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. explain why procurement through reimbursement basis was still allowed; and
b. instruct the City Accountant to stop processing DVs for reimbursement with incomplete supporting documents; and
c. further improve their measures to minimize, if not eliminate, these disbursements paid through reimbursement basis as regular and recurring expenses should not be paid through reimbursement basis to comply with the stated provisions of law aside from ensuring that the National Treasury receive the mandatory tax collections from business enterprises; and
d. submit to the Audit Team all the lacking documents needed to support the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted partially the lacking documents and supporting documents. The City Accountant minimized the practice of reimbursement and measures to collect withholding taxes will be enforced.
observations.reason: Not all lacking documents were submitted; reimbursement practice was minimized but not completely eliminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to facilitate the immediate submission of the 87 DVs involving a total amount of P10,502,463.78 as well as the 26 DVs covering CYs 2015 to 2017 transactions amounting to P151,550.85 together with the supporting documents, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit. Henceforth, require submission of accounts within the period prescribed under Section 100 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006, and in instances of unjustified failure to submit accounts, avail of the remedies and sanctions provided in Section 122 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office and City Treasurer’s Office submitted 87 DVs involving a total amount of P10,502,463.78. The Management submitted partially the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: As of August 13, 2020, there are 40 unsubmitted DVs totaling P763,458.56 covering CYs 2015 to 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. direct the City Accountant to submit immediately all Liquidation Reports together with the supporting documents, in compliance with the provisions of the PD No. 1445, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit; 
b. initiate disciplinary actions against those officials responsible for the disbursements not covered with Liquidation Reports and/or without complete documentations; and
c. require the Accountant to cause the refund of the total amount of cash advance which were not covered with Liquidation Reports and not duly documented.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant was able to submit partially the Liquidation Reports and its supporting documents. The Management withheld the salaries of the officials responsible for the liquidation of the cash advances. The City Accountant submitted partially the lacking documents and the supporting documents are being collated by Management.
observations.reason: Not all liquidation reports were submitted; recording of liquidation reports were still pending and collation of supporting documents not complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the immediate submission of the required documents, in compliance with the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184, Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documents were already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant not to process the payments for Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) with incomplete supporting documents;
b. the designated official drivers of government vehicles to properly accomplish Driver's Trip Tickets and Monthly Reports of Official Travels and the designated official to sign and approve the Reports and thereafter, adhere strictly to Sections 2, 4 and 7 of the Manual on the Use of Government Motor Vehicles; and
c. the City General Services Officer or her equivalent officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant hold the processing of DVs with incomplete supporting documents; Submitted the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. require the City Accountant to adhere to the above-cited provisions on reimbursement basis and stop processing reimbursements; 
b. instruct concerned officials to minimize the practice; and
c. submit to the Audit Team all lacking documents needed to support the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant stopped processing payments with incomplete supporting documents and minimized the practice of reimbursement. Lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reimbursement practice was minimized but some reimbursement disbursements still occurred; not all lacking documents were fully supplied.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor:
a. direct the City Accountant to adhere to the cited provisions on charging of Mobile Expenses against government funds;
b. initiate the passing of the Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution setting the limit on charging of mobile expenses against government funds; and
c. instruct the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the lacking documents needed to support the disbursements including a list of mobile phone users to complete the audit.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant minimized the processing of DVs with incomplete documentary requirements. Drafted the Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution setting the limit on charging of mobile expenses against government funds. Lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution was not yet enacted; some documents were still being collated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Secretariat of the Bids and Awards Committee to submit copies of Contracts/POs and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within five days upon perfection; 
b. City General Services Officer to furnish the Audit Team with Notice of Deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries; and
c. SPTC Administrator to furnish the COA Auditor with copies of perfected Lease Agreements entered into by the City Government of San Pedro with the lessees.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, Notice of Deliveries were submitted within five days upon perfection. The SPTC Administrator submitted the lease contracts to the Audit Team. Lease Agreements, Contract of Services, Job Orders and Consultancy Contracts were still not submitted in some instances but were subsequently provided as applicable.
observations.reason: Some documents such as Job Order, Consultancy contracts and certain Contracts were not yet submitted to the Auditor within five days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to adopt additional measures and strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate more income; coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of Real Property Tax Program to maximize collection of taxes; and avail of remedies and sanctions provided in RA No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Management isamenable with the recommendations and will implement additional measures to intensify tax collection and coordinate with barangays.
observations.reason: Not all delinquent accounts were yet collected; Notices of Delinquency were issued but full collection remains to be pursued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the SPTC Administrator/Market Committee to cancel/terminate the Contract of Lease of stallholders that sub-leased their stalls, in compliance with Section 14 of Article VI of the 2012 Revised Municipal Revenue Code of the City of San Pedro, and to issue Contracts of Lease to the 12 actual occupants of the stalls.
observations.managementAction: The SPTC Administrator submitted one year contracts, renewable every year, to its lessees. The SPTC Administrator/Market Committee issued one year contracts to the lessees. Notices and contracts were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Job Order, Consultancy contracts and some Lease Agreements were still not submitted to the Audit Team within five days upon perfection in prior instances, but SPTC has since issued contracts to actual stall occupants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor create an inspectorate committee as required under Section 118 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and stop the practice of requiring the City IAS Unit to conduct inspection as part of their regular function.
observations.managementAction: A team was created to facilitate the processing. Creation of the inspectorate committee is ongoing. Executive Order for the creation is subject for final review.
observations.reason: Executive Order for the creation of the Inspectorate Committee is not yet established by the City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Head of GSO to file sufficient bond, in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates set by the Bureau of Treasury, to ensure that the City will be sufficiently indemnified in case of losses due to theft; and
b. City Accountant and City Treasurer to stop the practice of granting cash advances to employees who are not properly bonded.
observations.managementAction: The Head of GSO filed for sufficient bond as property officer. The Management minimized the practice of granting cash advances to employees who are not properly bonded.
observations.reason: There were accountable officers that were not properly bonded; filing of bonds and full compliance still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor direct the LCPC to fully utilize the amount of one percent of the IRA for the utilization and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children, pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: The City has implemented some programs, projects and activities of the LCPC. The LCPC implemented certain activities and will seek to fully utilize the fund.
observations.reason: The LCPC fund was not optimally utilized with 17.72 percent utilization rate for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned officials to provide the Audit Team with updated information on the status of the PPAs in order to allow timely validation and reporting; and henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The Quarterly Report on Government Projects, Programs and Activities (GPPAs) was submitted to the Audit Team. Monitored the utilization of funds through preparation of the monthly report on utilization of funds.
observations.reason: The notice to the Audit Team upon posting of signages was not complied with in some instances.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor:
a. cause the maximum utilization of the 20% Development Fund by seeing to it that procurement requirements are promptly met so that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents of the City and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; 
b. employ regular monitoring mechanisms in meeting assessing the rate of progress of project implementation; and
c. maintain a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring of the implementation of the projects to ensure that programmed projects are completed on time.
observations.managementAction: The City has implemented some of the planned development projects. The Local Development Council conducted regular meetings to discuss the implementation and status of projects. Quarterly preparation of GPPA report was made to determine the status of projects.
observations.reason: The City still was not able to maximize the utilization of 20% Development Fund and some procurement requirements delayed project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor:
a. cause the full utilization of the fund so that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents; 
b. employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation; and
c. maintain a closer coordination among officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring so that programmed projects are completed on time.
observations.managementAction: The City implemented various projects funded by SEF. The Local School Board conducted meetings and monthly/quarterly utilization reports were being reviewed.
observations.reason: SEF funds were not optimally utilized due to additional funding needed for some projects and pending finalization/location inspections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to submit the required attachments of the City’s Annual Accomplishment Report as provided under Section 4.1.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted the HGDG Tests to the Audit Team. Lacking attachments were being compiled and some were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Only the HGDG test was submitted; other attachments were not yet provided at time of report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor fully utilize the GAD Budget by providing a system of monitoring the implementation of the GAD planned activities to promote gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The City implemented various GAD-related projects. The GAD Focal Person followed PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 schedule and committed to comply with COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the future. Some monitoring mechanisms were put in place.
observations.reason: GAD fund was not optimally utilized with 78.75 percent utilization rate for CY 2019; GFPS monitoring needs strengthening.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor a certified Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month using the format prescribed under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO and City Accountant prepared and submitted the monthly utilization report for LDRRMF to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to see to it that monitoring scheme is in place to ensure full and timely implementation of LDRRMF PPAs to protect the community from disasters, in line with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73.
observations.managementAction: Monitored the utilization of funds through preparation of the monthly report on utilization of funds. The Management will comply with the guidelines in preparation of LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP).
observations.reason: The 70 percent Mitigation fund was not optimally utilized with 88.45 percent utilization rate for CY 2019; monitoring scheme and full implementation still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
a. immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor all unsubmitted supporting documents, in order to avoid suspension/disallowance of the payment of P40,000,000.00; and
b. henceforth, ensure that payments for garbage collection, hauling and disposal services are completely documented, in compliance with Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted the lacking documents to the Audit Team and will ensure that future payments are supported with complete documentations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:
a. Barangay Officials to look for conducive locations for the Material Recovery Facilities so that all barangays have MRFs, in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168; and

action.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the City Mayor direct the City

with outstanding cash advances, and to strictly monitor the granting and
utilization of cash advances;

including those AOs who have retired or already out of service; and

liquidation of their cash advances.

settle their overdue cash advances, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997.

materials; and

Issued (SSMI); and

a. maintain Stock Cards for supplies;
b. prepare and submit monthly the SSMI to the City Accountant for the
recording of issuances; and

to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given
date.

Treasurer to stop granting cash advances to elected officials and coterminous
employee, in compliance with COA Circular 2012-001.

require submission of accounts within the period prescribed under Section 100 of
PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 2009-006, and in instances of unjustified
failure to submit accounts, avail of the remedies provided in Section 122 of PD No.
1445.

We reiterated our previous recommendationsthat the City Mayor:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE strengthen the LGU GFPS by completing the composition of the GFPS Execom and TWG to ensure that the LGU GFPS could fulfill its tasks.
We reiterated that the management establish the GAD Database that will strengthen the planning and budgeting processes for GAD and ensure that planned PPAs address identified gender issues, that objectives, performance indicators and targets are well-formulated to facilitate monitoring and evaluation for the attainment of GAD objectives.
We recommended the formulation and legislation of the GAD Code through the coordination of the City Administration and the Sangguniang Panlungsod in order to support the LGU in addressing gender issues.
We recommended that mainstreaming of GAD-related activities in all department/offices of the City be implemented by the Management.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to plan and implement projects and programs that will directly benefit women. Consider the potentials and borne skills of women in the formulation of strategies and bases that will be used in designing priority projects and programs for GAD Plan responsive to its prescribed thrust and vision; and the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget. In addition, strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the plan was fully implemented.
